segments.text: Why is this juice brand worth 500 crores? Because they are not selling drinks. They're selling your childhood.
Every design detail, even the logo. It's a psychological trick to trigger your nostalgia. Most brands try to look modern and futuristic, but paper boat
used flat colors exactly like a school notebooks. Even their logo is a simple paper boat. Why? Because it's a universal symbol of childhood innocence
and rainy days. It doesn't look like a corporate brand. It looks like a memory.
But why use floppy pouches instead of premium bottles? See, that's the genius part. The matte finish feels like a paper. When you touch it, your brain
instantly connects that physical touch to the name and the logo. Great design isn't about making something expensive. It's about making you feel something.
